key_points: Tenali Rama was a clever poet and advisor in the court of King Krishnadeva Raya
A misunderstanding occurred when the queen yawned while the king was reciting his poem
The king believed the queen was insulting him and became angry
Tenali Rama used intelligence and tact to explain the situation to the king
His clever reasoning helped the king understand that yawning is a natural action
examples: The queen’s yawn was mistaken by the king as disrespect
Tenali Rama used the example of paddy seeds to indirectly explain the truth
Instead of directly criticizing the king, Tenali Rama spoke cleverly to protect the king’s dignity
The king realized his mistake after hearing Tenali Rama’s explanation
Harmony returned to the palace after the misunderstanding was cleared
formulas_or_processes: Situation: The queen yawns during the king’s poem
Conflict: The king misunderstands the action and becomes angry
Intervention: Tenali Rama explains the situation through a clever example
Outcome: The king realizes his mistake and apologizes
exam_tips: Understand how Tenali Rama used wit and intelligence instead of direct criticism
Remember the reason behind the conflict between the king and the queen
Focus on the example Tenali Rama used to make the king realize the truth
Learn the moral of the story: wisdom and tact can resolve misunderstandings peacefully
